findings.trigger: Have today's daily word recorded, no write-slot tickets, and buy a lootbox-only amount >= threshold with ticketQuantity=0. Call requestLootboxRng(), do not fulfill, wait 6h, then call retryLootboxRng(): it reverts because LR_MID_DAY was never set.
findings.reasoning: The real counterexample is retry reachability, not wrong-branch fulfillment. requestLootboxRng sets LR_MID_DAY only when a nonempty ticket write slot is swapped (contracts/modules/DegenerusGameAdvanceModule.sol:1127-1132), but still requests VRF, advances LR_INDEX, and stores vrfRequestId/rngRequestTime (contracts/modules/DegenerusGameAdvanceModule.sol:1136-1142). A lootbox-only buy skips ticket queuing when ticketCost==0 while still adding pending ETH (contracts/modules/DegenerusGameMintModule.sol:1548-1564, contracts/modules/DegenerusGameMintModule.sol:1609-1613). retryLootboxRng then permanently rejects that stalled request because LR_MID_DAY==0 (contracts/modules/DegenerusGameAdvanceModule.sol:1152-1159). This is not a permanent brick: after the daily timeout, rngGate can replace the stale shared request and later finalize LR_INDEX-1 through the daily path (contracts/modules/DegenerusGameAdvanceModule.sol:1265-1270, contracts/modules/DegenerusGameAdvanceModule.sol:1677-1688, contracts/modules/DegenerusGameAdvanceModule.sol:1837-1847, contracts/modules/DegenerusGameAdvanceModule.sol:1280-1284). rawFulfillRandomWords itself is still requestId/coordinator gated, so I did not find a wrong raw callback branch.

The partial-drain state is resumable. Same-day mid-day drains require the reserved LR_INDEX-1 word before processing and clear LR_MID_DAY only once the read slot finishes (contracts/modules/DegenerusGameAdvanceModule.sol:218-245). processTicketBatch resumes by ticketLevel/ticketCursor and persists the cursor only on partial stop (contracts/modules/DegenerusGameMintModule.sol:851-916). Fresh daily/mid-day requests are blocked while the daily lock or LR_MID_DAY is active, so LR_INDEX does not move across chunks (contracts/modules/DegenerusGameAdvanceModule.sol:1078-1083, contracts/modules/DegenerusGameAdvanceModule.sol:1152-1159). The subscriber/backfill split and gap/backfill split leave dailyIdx unadvanced so the next call resumes the intended work (contracts/modules/DegenerusGameAdvanceModule.sol:347-354, contracts/modules/DegenerusGameAdvanceModule.sol:387-397). The recorded-but-unsealed clamp prevents wall-day jumps from skipping the next recorded day (contracts/modules/DegenerusGameAdvanceModule.sol:176-186).

Post-request placements bind the live index, not the in-flight word. requestLootboxRng advances LR_INDEX after the VRF request succeeds (contracts/modules/DegenerusGameAdvanceModule.sol:1136-1139), and fulfillment writes LR_INDEX-1 (contracts/modules/DegenerusGameAdvanceModule.sol:1840-1847). Later purchases/bets read the current LR_INDEX; presale boxes and Degenerette also reject already-worded live indices (contracts/modules/DegenerusGameMintModule.sol:1584-1601, contracts/modules/DegenerusGameMintModule.sol:1949-1951, contracts/modules/DegenerusGameDegeneretteModule.sol:531-533). Resolvers require the stored index word to be nonzero (contracts/modules/DegenerusGameLootboxModule.sol:541-545, contracts/modules/DegenerusGameDegeneretteModule.sol:648-653), and openHumanBoxes stops at an unworded index instead of advancing past it (contracts/modules/DegenerusGameLootboxModule.sol:679-682). Outcome seeds use the committed word and immutable inputs, not live day, for human/direct/redemption outcomes; lootbox boon outcome uses seed bits, while currentDay is used for boon application/expiry metadata (contracts/modules/DegenerusGameLootboxModule.sol:556-560, contracts/modules/DegenerusGameLootboxModule.sol:880-887, contracts/modules/DegenerusGameLootboxModule.sol:968-974, contracts/modules/DegenerusGameLootboxModule.sol:1427, contracts/modules/DegenerusGameLootboxModule.sol:1459-1468).

A fulfillment cannot land on the wrong rawFulfillRandomWords branch under honest governance: the callback requires the current coordinator, current requestId, and rngWordCurrent==0, then branches solely on rngLockedFlag (contracts/modules/DegenerusGameAdvanceModule.sol:1827-1847). Daily requests set rngLockedFlag in _finalizeRngRequest (contracts/modules/DegenerusGameAdvanceModule.sol:1692-1695); mid-day requestLootboxRng does not set it and is blocked while daily lock is active (contracts/modules/DegenerusGameAdvanceModule.sol:1078-1083).

I did not find an outcome-changing advance/rebind. Fresh mid-day and fresh daily requests advance LR_INDEX once so new placements go to the next index (contracts/modules/DegenerusGameAdvanceModule.sol:1136-1139, contracts/modules/DegenerusGameAdvanceModule.sol:1685-1688). Retry and rotation preserve the already-reserved index (contracts/modules/DegenerusGameAdvanceModule.sol:1166-1169, contracts/modules/DegenerusGameAdvanceModule.sol:1768-1772). The MIDRNG-01 no-ticket stalled case can skip a daily index advance because the shared request slots make isRetry true, but that leaves post-request placements unworded for a later request rather than resolving them with the in-flight word.

The clamp covers the recorded-but-unsealed window. If wall day advances beyond dailyIdx+1 while rngWordByDay[dailyIdx+1] is already recorded, advanceGame clamps day back to dailyIdx+1 (contracts/modules/DegenerusGameAdvanceModule.sol:176-186). rngGate then returns the recorded word instead of requesting/reusing a different word (contracts/modules/DegenerusGameAdvanceModule.sol:1201-1203), and _unlockRng is the only day-seal that advances dailyIdx and clears the lock (contracts/modules/DegenerusGameAdvanceModule.sol:1793-1802).

No permanent maroon or double-resolve found. openHumanBoxes never advances past a zero word (contracts/modules/DegenerusGameLootboxModule.sol:679-682) and only persists the cursor after the loop (contracts/modules/DegenerusGameLootboxModule.sol:722-728). Gap recovery backfills empty indices below LR_INDEX until it reaches a filled index (contracts/modules/DegenerusGameAdvanceModule.sol:1217-1223, contracts/modules/DegenerusGameAdvanceModule.sol:1888-1900). Already-filled daily finalization is idempotent (contracts/modules/DegenerusGameAdvanceModule.sol:1280-1284), and stale raw callbacks are rejected by requestId mismatch (contracts/modules/DegenerusGameAdvanceModule.sol:1831-1832).

overallVerdict: Static review of contracts/ tree 4a67209a found no catastrophic mid-day brick and no outcome-changing word rebind. One reachable non-catastrophic counterexample exists: stalled mid-day requests that did not swap a ticket buffer cannot use retryLootboxRng because LR_MID_DAY is not set; they recover through the later daily timeout path instead.
